SYLLABUS: DESCRIPTION

This course provides an introduction to the accounting process and the preparation of financial statements for service and merchandising businesses. It covers the recording, classifying, and summarizing of business transactions.

This course applies economic principles and methodologies to the decision-making process within an organization. It focuses on demand analysis, production and cost functions, and market structures to optimize business performance.

This course explores the nature of mathematics as a set of tools for understanding patterns and solving practical problems. It covers statistical tools for data analysis, interest and discounts, and mathematical logic relevant to business.

This course explores the theoretical foundation of financial reporting. It focuses on the Philippine Financial Reporting Standards (PFRS) and the IASB Framework for the preparation and presentation of financial statements.

This course involves an in-depth study of the recognition, measurement, and disclosure of assets (cash, receivables, inventories, and investments). It bridges basic accounting to complex professional standards.

This course examines the legal principles governing the nature, effects, and kinds of obligations. It also covers the essential elements, forms, and interpretation of contracts under the Civil Code of the Philippines.

This course is a continuation of the study of financial accounting, focusing on the recognition and measurement of liabilities and equity. It includes topics on employee benefits, income taxes, and the preparation of the Statement of Changes in Equity.

This course focuses on the systematic recording and analysis of costs for materials, labor, and overhead. It covers job-order costing, process costing, and standard costing systems used in manufacturing and service industries.

This course introduces the principles of taxation and the Philippine tax system. It covers the computation of taxable income and tax liabilities for individuals and corporations, including deductions and exemptions.

This course concludes the intermediate financial accounting series, focusing on complex financial reporting issues. Key topics include accounting for income taxes, employee benefits (pensions), leases, and the full preparation of the Statement of Cash Flows and related footnote disclosures.

This course explores the integration of accounting principles with information technology. It focuses on the design, implementation, and internal control of systems that process financial data, ensuring the integrity and security of business information for decision-making.

This course examines the legal framework governing business organizations in the Philippines. It covers the laws on Partnership and Corporations, including their formation, operation, dissolution, and the rights and duties of members.

This course introduces the objective and general principles of an audit of financial statements. It covers the professional ethics, legal liabilities of auditors, and the fundamental concepts of risk and internal control.

This course introduces the principles of financial management, focusing on wealth maximization. It covers financial statement analysis, capital budgeting, working capital management, and the risk-return trade-off in investment decisions.

BSCS: Governance, Business Ethics, and Risk Management
SYLLABUS: This course explores the principles of corporate governance, social responsibility, and ethical behavior in business. It focuses on the role of the Board of Directors and the management of risks that impact organizational integrity.

BSCS: Auditing and Assurance: Concepts and Applications 1
SYLLABUS: This course applies auditing standards to the audit of the revenue and receipt cycle, expenditure cycle, and production cycle. It focuses on gathering audit evidence and documenting audit workpapers.
This course covers specialized accounting topics such as partnership formation and liquidation, joint arrangements, insurance contracts, and accounting for non-profit organizations.

BSCS: Regulatory Framework and Legal Issues in Business
SYLLABUS: This course deals with the legal aspects of various business transactions and regulatory requirements. It includes laws on negotiable instruments, insurance, cooperatives, and other special laws relevant to the accountancy profession.

BSCS: Auditing and Assurance: Concepts and Applications 2
SYLLABUS: This course applies auditing procedures to the audit of the financing and investing cycles. It focuses on the audit of long-term assets, liabilities, equity accounts, and the finalization of the audit through the issuance of an audit report.
This course focuses on complex accounting for business unions, such as mergers, acquisitions, and the preparation of consolidated financial statements for parent and subsidiary relationships.

This course provides students with the skills to conduct independent research in the field of accounting. It covers problem identification, literature review, methodology, and the presentation of research findings.
This course serves as a capstone where students apply financial and non-financial data to strategic decision-making. It integrates management, marketing, and finance to solve complex business cases.

This course explores the impact of Computer Information Systems (CIS) on the auditing process. It focuses on the risks, controls, and specialized audit techniques used when financial data is processed electronically.

This course provides students with actual work experience in an accounting firm, government agency, or private corporation. It aims to apply classroom theories to real-world professional practice and develop workplace competencies.
